# API Discovery and Extract

> ⚠️ **Stop. This document is incomplete by itself.**
> Before implementing anything, read [`AGENT_GUIDE.md`](../AGENT_GUIDE.md).
> It contains global rules, required namespaces, architecture constraints, and documentation navigation.
> Do not continue without reading it.

> You are here if you need to:
> - find the exact LinqToDB API for the installed package version
> - decide whether a typed API exists before using a generic fallback
> - inspect overload families, return types, XML-doc remarks, or AI-Tags
> - answer provider-specific API questions without inventing methods

---

## Core Rule

Markdown guides explain concepts, constraints, and common mistakes. They are not a complete public
API listing.

The package XML documentation is the exact current-version public API surface for members that have
XML comments:

```text
lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml
```

Missing from markdown does not mean missing from LinqToDB. Do not conclude that an API is
unavailable until XML-doc has been searched.

If this package also ships a generated markdown API extract, use it as a search aid. The canonical
API shape is still the XML-doc member entry.

`docs/api.md` is allowed to contain curated API extract sections generated from XML-doc. Those
sections are optimized for discovery, not for copying every XML member verbatim.

When `linq2db.xml` changes, the generated API extract in this file must be regenerated in the same
change. Do not manually patch generated extract rows except as a temporary diagnostic step; fix the
XML-doc source or the extract generator and regenerate.

---

## Discovery Workflow

Use this workflow for any API-level question:

1. Read the relevant task guide in `docs/*.md`.
2. Start with the narrowest and most specific API surface: provider-specific guides, maps,
   namespaces, and typed helpers.
3. Search the generated API extract by its `Search anchors:` lines first. Anchors are the primary
   discovery vocabulary for agents: they include provider names, SQL keywords, common task words,
   helper-name fragments, receiver scope, and AI-Tags.
4. Use headings, summaries, and AI-Tags to confirm likely candidates.
5. Only after a candidate is found, copy the `XML member` id from its table row.
6. Search `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml` by that exact XML member id.
7. Verify the exact member signature, receiver type, parameters, return type, remarks, and tags.
8. Prefer typed and provider-specific APIs found in XML-doc.
9. Use generic string-based APIs only when no typed API exists or when the typed API does not cover
   the requested case.
10. Use custom SQL, raw SQL, and interceptors only after typed and generic APIs do not cover the
   requested case.
11. If markdown and XML-doc disagree, prefer XML-doc for exact API shape and mention the discrepancy.

Do not invent APIs, overloads, options, provider flags, provider capabilities, XML-doc remarks, or
AI-Tags.

For SQL hint questions, this workflow has an additional gate: search `docs/hints-api-map.md`
and the provider `*Hints` XML-doc members before recommending `QueryHint`, `TableHint`,
`Sql.Expression`, raw SQL, or interceptors.

For negative hint lookups, be stricter than normal retrieval. Do not conclude that a typed helper is
absent from `docs/hints-api-map.md` unless you searched that map by exact provider and exact SQL or
database term. Then search `Search anchors:` in this file for the provider `*Hints` type, SQL term,
candidate helper fragments, `Group=Hints`, and `HintType=...` before using generic fallback APIs.

Hint lookup order is strict: map first, XML-doc verification second, typed provider helper third,
generic hint APIs fourth, custom SQL/interceptors last.
This applies even when the database feature is a table modifier, lock clause, query directive, or
provider-specific SQL extension rather than a classic optimizer hint.

---

## API Extract Contract

The agent-friendly API extract is intentionally more compact than `linq2db.xml`.

It should help agents find the right API family quickly, then verify exact details in XML-doc.
Do not treat a compact extract entry as the only overload that exists.
Do not use raw XML member ids as the first search vocabulary. They are exact lookup keys after a
candidate is found, not human- or agent-friendly retrieval text.

The generated extract is derived data. Its source of truth is `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml`, which in
turn is produced from public XML comments. Any change that affects XML-doc members, summaries,
remarks, parameter documentation, or AI-Tags must also refresh this extract before the package is
shipped.

The generated extract must include only LinqToDB public API members whose XML-doc member id starts
with `T:LinqToDB.`, `M:LinqToDB.`, `P:LinqToDB.`, `F:LinqToDB.`, or `E:LinqToDB.`. Do not include
external or compatibility members such as `System.*`, `Microsoft.*`, `JetBrains.*`, or
`BitOperations` as API entries. Those names may still appear inside LinqToDB member signatures as
parameter or return types.

Each extract entry should prefer this shape:

```md
### TypeName.MemberName

Search anchors: provider term, SQL term, common user wording, AI-Tags terms.
Namespace: LinqToDB...
Member kind: extension method / method / type / property / attribute.
Primary receivers: ITable<T>, IDataContext, ...
Returns: ...
AI-Tags: ...

Overloads:
| Form | Receiver | Required arguments | Optional arguments | Returns | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Remarks:
- Important XML-doc behavior that changes how code should be written.
- Version/provider/configuration constraints.
```

### Overload Policy

Do not paste every overload as a separate long section when overloads differ only by optional
parameters, sync/async shape, expression delegate type, cancellation token, or provider marker type.
Group them under one API family entry and list their forms in an overload table.

The extract should include enough overload information for discovery:

- receiver type, especially for extension methods;
- required arguments;
- meaningful optional arguments;
- return type;
- async vs sync distinction;
- provider-specific receiver or marker type;
- important generic constraints when XML-doc exposes them.

If the exact overload needed by the task is not shown in the compact extract, this does not mean it
is absent. Continue to `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml` and search the API family, member name, receiver
type, and parameter names before falling back to a generic API.

### Generic Parameter Policy

Generic parameters are important for exact C# code, but too much generic noise hurts retrieval.

Use this rule:

- keep generic arity or common generic names in signatures and overload tables;
- include generic receiver types such as `ITable<TSource>` or `IQueryable<T>` because they define
  where an extension method can be called;
- avoid putting long generic parameter lists in retrieval anchors unless the generic name is the
  thing users search for;
- do not create separate extract entries only because generic parameter names differ;
- verify exact generic parameters and constraints in XML-doc before writing final code when they
  matter.

Good heading:

```md
### ProviderHints.SomeHint
```

Good overload row:

```md
| `SomeHint<TSource>(this IProviderSpecificTable<TSource> table)` | `IProviderSpecificTable<TSource>` | table | none | `IProviderSpecificTable<TSource>` | Adds a provider table hint to one table source. |
```

Avoid using the generic-heavy signature as the only retrieval anchor. Agents should be able to find
the entry by provider name, SQL hint text, helper name, receiver scope, and `Group=Hints`.

---

## Search Strategy

Search specific surfaces first, then broaden only when no match is found.

Ordering is part of the documentation contract. If a guide or extract lists a typed/provider-specific
path before a generic API, custom SQL, raw SQL, or interceptor path, treat the earlier path as the
preferred path to verify first.

| Task | Search for |
|---|---|
| Provider-specific API | Provider namespace, provider marker (`AsSqlServer`, `AsClickHouse`, etc.), provider `*Hints`/`*Tools`/extension type names |
| SQL hints | SQL hint text, `Group=Hints`, `HintType=...`, provider `*Hints` type, and `docs/hints-api-map.md` |
| Configuration | `DataOptions`, `UseXxx`, option name, provider name |
| Mapping | Attribute name, fluent method name, `MappingSchema`, `Configuration`, `DataType`, `DbType` |
| DML/query extension | Operation name, extension method name, `Group=DML`, `Group=Merge`, receiver type |
| Async API | Method name plus `Async`; remember async query/DML methods often require `using LinqToDB.Async;` |

When searching XML-doc, include both the user-facing SQL/database term and likely LinqToDB naming.
Provider-specific helpers do not always map one-to-one to SQL spelling.

---

## Fallbacks

Generic APIs are valid tools, but they are not proof that a typed API is absent.

Do not stop API discovery at a generic method just because it is documented. Generic methods are
fallbacks after typed/provider-specific lookup.

Before recommending these APIs, search XML-doc for a typed or provider-specific member:

- `QueryHint(...)`
- `TableHint(...)`
- `Sql.Expression`
- `Sql.Extension`
- `Sql.Table`
- raw SQL command text
- SQL text rewriting through interceptors

Use them when the typed API is missing, too narrow, or intentionally not available for the
requested provider feature.

---

## AI-Tags

Some XML-doc remarks contain `AI-Tags`. Use them as machine-readable classification, not as a
replacement for the full member documentation.

For hints:

- `HintType` tells where the hint is applied (`Table`, `TablesInScope`, `Join`, `Query`, etc.).
- When a provider-specific `Table` hint exists, also search for a matching `TablesInScope` hint for
  the same provider and SQL hint text before answering.
- For "several tables", "all tables", "whole query", or "scope" requests, search
  `HintType=TablesInScope` and `*InScope*` provider helpers before generic
  `TablesInScopeHint(...)`. Apply the scope helper to the query/subquery that already contains the
  target table references; applying it to only the first `ITable<T>` before adding joins affects
  only that source.
- Treat method-name patterns as search hints only. Common shapes are `<Base>Hint` ->
  `<Base>InScopeHint` and `With<Base>` -> `With<Base>InScope`. Verify the exact name in
  `docs/hints-api-map.md` and XML-doc; do not invent scope-helper names by string concatenation.
- The concrete SQL hint text is documented in the member XML summary, usually inside `<c>...</c>`.
- `docs/hints-api-map.md` is a reverse lookup built from that XML-doc shape. Use it to find
  candidate typed helpers, then verify exact signatures in XML-doc.
- The receiver type matters. A table hint helper and a query/scope hint helper can have similar
  names but different extension receiver types.

This section is generated from the package XML documentation and optimized for agent retrieval.

Missing from this compact section is not proof that an API or overload is absent. Search XML-doc before falling back to generic APIs.

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CompareNulls, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls` | If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ConcatenateOrderBy, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: . Example: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DisableQueryCache, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreloadGroups` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group . Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationAlias, Association, Alias, SQL, SELECT, FROM, LEFT, JOIN.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias` | Format for association alias. Default value: "a_{0}". In the following query var query = from child in db.Child select new { child.ChildID, child.Parent.Value1 }; for association Parent will be generated association A_Parent in resulting SQL. SELECT [child].[ChildID], [a_Parent].[Value1] FROM [Child] [child] LEFT JOIN [Parent] [a_Parent] ON ([child].[ParentID] = [a_Parent].[ParentID]) Set this value to to disable special alias generation queries. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy, Enable, Constant, Expression, Order, ORDER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy` | If , linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateFinalAliases, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child] |  |

| `P:LinqToDB.Configuration.IDataProviderSettings.Attributes` | Extra provider-specific parameters. Sybase: assemblyName - Sybase provider assembly name. SAP HANA: assemblyName - SAP HANA provider assembly name. Oracle: assemblyName - Oracle provider assembly name. SQL Server: assemblyName - provider assembly name. Recognized values: "System.Data", "System.Data.SqlClient", "Microsoft.Data.SqlClient". version - T-SQL support level, recognized values: "2005", "2008", "2012", "2014", "2016", "2017", "2018". Default: "2008". DB2: version - DB2 platform, recognized values: "zOS" or "z/OS" - DB2 for z/OS. Default platform - DB2 LUW. |  |

To reset timeout to provider/connection defaults use or methods |  |

### LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: FromSql, From, Sql, Group=Helpers, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=QueryRoot, LINQ, SQL, SELECT, FROM, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,LinqToDB.RawSqlString,System.Object[])` | Creates a LINQ query based on a raw SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name); As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include parameter place holders in the SQL query string and then supply parameter values as additional arguments. Any parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM [dbo].[SearchBlogs]({0})", userSuppliedSearchTerm); This overload also accepts DbParameter instances as parameter values. context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM [dbo].[SearchBlogs]({0})", new DataParameter("@searchTerm", userSuppliedSearchTerm, DataType.Int64)); | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.FormattableString)` | Creates a LINQ query based on an interpolated string representing a SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name); As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include interpolated parameter place holders in the SQL query string. Any interpolated parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - context.FromSql<Blogs>($"SELECT * FROM [dbo].[SearchBlogs]({userSuppliedSearchTerm})"); | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |

### LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSqlScalar

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: FromSqlScalar, From, Sql, Scalar, Group=Helpers, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=QueryRoot, LINQ, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSqlScalar``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.FormattableString)` | Creates a LINQ query based on an interpolated string representing a SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name); As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include interpolated parameter place holders in the SQL query string. Any interpolated parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - context.FromSqlScalar<Blogs>($"UNNEST({array})"); An alias for the scalar value will be generated automatically with the name value. For most databases, the following SQL will be generated: SELECT t.value FROM (SELECT '1') AS t(value) For databases that do not support this syntax, ensure that your query provides an alias for the column named value to allow correct translation, for example: SELECT t.value FROM (SELECT '1' AS value) AS t | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseCompareNulls

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseCompareNulls, Use, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseCompareNulls(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.CompareNulls)` | If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseConcatenateOrderBy, Use, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseConcatenateOrderBy(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: . Example: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDisableQueryCache

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseDisableQueryCache, Use, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDisableQueryCache(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDoNotClearOrderBys

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseDoNotClearOrderBys, Use, Not, Clear, Order, Bys, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDoNotClearOrderBys(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: - if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: . |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseEnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseEnableContextSchemaEdit, Use, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseEnableContextSchemaEdit(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schems (). Otherwise, will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseExponentialBase(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Double)` | The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: 2. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseGenerateExpressionTest, Use, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateExpressionTest(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseGenerateFinalAliases, Use, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateFinalAliases(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child] |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGuardGrouping(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if - will be thrown for such queries; - if - eager loading used. Default value: . |  |

|---|---|---|

Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptor

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseInterceptor, Use, Interceptor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptor(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor)` | Adds instance to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. Extensions can also register multiple s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseInterceptors, Use, Interceptors.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor[])` | Adds instances to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. Extensions can also register multiple s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor})` | Adds instances to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. A single interceptor instance can implement multiple different interceptor interfaces. It will be registered as an interceptor for all interfaces that it implements. Extensions can also register multiple s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseMemberTranslator, Use, Member, Translator, NET, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator)` | Adds instance to those registered on the context. Translators can be used to translate .NET member expressions into SQL expressions during query translation. This is the low-level extensibility point behind provider or application-specific method translation. Prefer [Sql.Expression], [Sql.Function], or [ExpressionMethod] for simpler cases; use when you need direct access to translation context and SQL-expression creation. See package-local docs/custom-sql.md and docs/configuration.md for guidance on choosing between these extension points. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator})` | Adds collection instance to those registered on the context. Translators can be used to translate .NET member expressions into SQL expressions during query translation. Registration order matters when multiple translators can handle the same member. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithCompareNulls

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithCompareNulls, With, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithCompareNulls(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,LinqToDB.CompareNulls)` | If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithConcatenateOrderBy, With, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithConcatenateOrderBy(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: . Example: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithDisableQueryCache, With, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

Default value: . |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableContextSchemaEdit(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schems (). Otherwise, will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateExpressionTest(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithGenerateFinalAliases, With, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,System.Boolean)` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child] |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode, Firebird, Identifier, Quote, Mode.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode` |  |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Auto, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto` | Quote identifiers if needed. LINQ To DB will quote identifiers, if they are not valid without quotation. This includes: - use of reserved words; - use of any characters except latin letters, digits, _ and $; - use digit, _ or $ as first character. This is default mode. Note that if you need to preserve casing of identifiers, you should use mode. Quoted identifiers not supported by SQL Dialect < 1. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: GetQueryParameterNormalizer, Get, Query, Parameter, Normalizer.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer` | Returns instance of , which implements normalization logic for parameters of single query. E.g. it could include: trimming names that are too longremoving/replacing unsupported charactersname deduplication for parameters with same name. For implementation without state it is recommended to return static instance. E.g. this could be done for providers with positional parameters that ignore names. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: InsertAll, Insert, All, INSERT, ALL, INTO, VALUES.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll` | This mode generates INSERT ALL statement. Note that INSERT ALL doesn't support sequences and will use single generated value for all rows. INSERT ALL INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/) ... INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/) |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: InsertDual, Insert, Dual, INSERT, SELECT, INTO, FROM, DUAL, UNION, ALL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual` | This mode generates INSERT ... SELECT statement. INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL UNION ALL ... UNION ALL SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto

Search anchors: InsertInto, Insert, Into, INSERT, INTO, VALUES.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto` | This mode performs regular INSERT INTO query with array of values for each column. INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) VALUES(:column1ArrayParameter, ..., :columnXArrayParameter) |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Needed` | Quote identifiers only when it is required according to PostgreSQL identifier quotation rules (except uppercase letters, for that look at mode): identifier use reserved wordidentifier contains whitespace character(s) |  |

| `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SQLite.SQLiteOptions.AlwaysCheckDbNull` | Enables null-value checks during database data mapping even if SQLite reports that column cannot be NULL to avoid on mapping when database reports nullability incorrectly. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: DataLength, Data, Length, DATALENGTH, SQL, NULL, LEN.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength``1(``0)` | DATALENGTH (Transact-SQL)This function returns the number of bytes used to represent any expression.DATALENGTH becomes really helpful when used with data types that can store variable-length data, such as: ntextnvarchartextvarbinaryvarchar For a NULL value, DATALENGTH returns NULL. Use the LEN to return the number of characters encoded into a given string expression, and DATALENGTH to return the size in bytes for a given string expression. These outputs may differ depending on the data type and type of encoding used in the column. For more information on storage differences between different encoding types, see Collation and Unicode Support. |  |

Use the LEN to return the number of characters encoded into a given string expression, and DATALENGTH to return the size in bytes for a given string expression. ### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAll

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableAll, Temporal, Table, All.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAll``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0})` | See Temporal tableExpressionALLQualifying Rows All rows Note Returns the union of rows that belong to the current and the history table. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAsOf

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableAsOf, Temporal, Table, AND.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAsOf``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime)` | See Temporal tableExpressionAS OFdateTimeQualifying RowsValidFrom <= dateTime AND ValidTo > dateTimeNote Returns a table with rows containing the values that were current at the specified point in time in the past. Internally, a union is performed between the temporal table and its history table and the results are filtered to return the values in the row that was valid at the point in time specified by the dateTime parameter. The value for a row is deemed valid if the system_start_time_column_name value is less than or equal to the dateTime parameter value and the system_end_time_column_name value is greater than the dateTime parameter value. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableBetween

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableBetween, Temporal, Table, Between, AND, FOR, SYSTEM_TIME.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableBetween``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See Temporal tableExpressionBETWEENdateTimeANDdateTime2Qualifying RowsValidFrom <= dateTime2 AND ValidTo > dateTimeNote Same as in the FOR SYSTEM_TIME FROMdateTimeTOdateTime2 description, except the table of rows returned includes rows that became active on the upper boundary defined by the dateTime2 endpoint. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableContainedIn

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableContainedIn, Temporal, Table, Contained, AND, CONTAINED.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableContainedIn``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See Temporal tableExpressionCONTAINED IN (dateTime, dateTime2) Qualifying RowsValidFrom >= dateTime AND ValidTo <= dateTime2Note Returns a table with the values for all row versions that were opened and closed within the specified time range defined by the two period values for the CONTAINED IN argument. Rows that became active exactly on the lower boundary or ceased being active exactly on the upper boundary are included. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableFromTo

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableFromTo, Temporal, Table, From, AND, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableFromTo``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See Temporal tableExpressionFROMdateTimeTOdateTime2Qualifying RowsValidFrom < dateTime2 AND ValidTo > dateTimeNote Returns a table with the values for all row versions that were active within the specified time range, regardless of whether they started being active before the dateTime parameter value for the FROM argument or ceased being active after the dateTime2 parameter value for the TO argument. Internally, a union is performed between the temporal table and its history table and the results are filtered to return the values for all row versions that were active at any time during the time range specified. Rows that stopped being active exactly on the lower boundary defined by the FROM endpoint aren't included, and records that became active exactly on the upper boundary defined by the TO endpoint are also not included. |  |

### LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ExpressionMethodAttribute, Expression, Method, Attribute, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute` | When applied to method or property, tells linq2db to replace them in queryable LINQ expression with another expression, returned by method, specified in this attribute. Requirements to expression method: - expression method should be in the same class and replaced property of method; - method could be private. When applied to property, expression: - method should return function expression with the same return type as property type; - expression method could take up to two parameters in any order - current object parameter and database connection context object. When applied to method: - expression method should return function expression with the same return type as method return type; - method cannot have void return type; - parameters in expression method should go in the same order as in substituted method; - expression could take method instance object as first parameter; - expression could take database connection context object as last parameter; - last method parameters could be ommited from expression method, but only if you don't add database connection context parameter. |  |

### LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute.Alias

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Alias.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute.Alias` | Gets or sets alias for substitution expression. Note that alias can be overriden by projection member name. |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: SetFieldReaderExpression, Set, Field, Reader, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.Boolean,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Int32,``1}})` | Sets the reader expression for a field of type in the data provider. var dataProvider = SqlServerTools.GetDataProvider(SqlServerVersion.v2019); dataProvider.SetFieldReaderExpression<SqlDataReader,decimal>((r, i) => GetDecimal(r, i)); static decimal GetDecimal(SqlDataReader rd, int index) { var value = rd.GetSqlDecimal(index); if (value.Precision > 29) { var str = value.ToString(); var val = decimal.Parse(str); return val; } return value.Value; } |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Int32,``1}})` | Sets the reader expression for a field of type in the data provider. var dataProvider = SqlServerTools.GetDataProvider(SqlServerVersion.v2019); dataProvider.SetFieldReaderExpression<SqlDataReader,decimal>((r, i) => GetDecimal(r, i)); static decimal GetDecimal(SqlDataReader rd, int index) { var value = rd.GetSqlDecimal(index); if (value.Precision > 29) { var str = value.ToString(); var val = decimal.Parse(str); return val; } return value.Value; } |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.String,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Int32,``1}})` | Sets the reader expression for a field of type in the data provider. var dataProvider = SqlServerTools.GetDataProvider(SqlServerVersion.v2019); dataProvider.SetFieldReaderExpression<DbDataReader,DateTime>("time", (r, i) => GetDateTimeAsTime(r.GetDateTime(i))); static DateTime GetDateTimeAsTime(DateTime value) { if (value is { Year: 1900, Month: 1, Day: 1 }) return new DateTime(1, 1, 1, value.Hour, value.Minute, value.Second, value.Millisecond); return value; } |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.Type,System.String,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Int32,``1}})` | Sets the reader expression for a field of type in the data provider. var dataProvider = SqlServerTools.GetDataProvider(SqlServerVersion.v2019); dataProvider.SetFieldReaderExpression<DbDataReader,string>(typeof(byte[]), "vector", (r, i) => r.GetString(i)); |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.DataProviderExtensions.SetFieldReaderExpression``2(LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider,System.Type,System.Type,System.Type,System.Type,System.String,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Int32,``1}})` |  |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Ydb.YdbTransientExceptionDetector

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: YdbTransientExceptionDetector, Ydb, Transient, Exception, Detector, YDB.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Ydb.YdbTransientExceptionDetector` | Detector for identifying whether an exception represents a transient YDB error, implemented without a hard dependency on Ydb.Sdk. Works by inspecting two properties of YdbException: bool IsTransient — indicates if the error is transient (temporary).enum Code — contains the YDB status code for the error. |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Ydb.YdbTransientExceptionDetector.ShouldRetryOn

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: ShouldRetryOn, Should, Retry, YDB, SDK.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.Ydb.YdbTransientExceptionDetector.ShouldRetryOn(System.Exception,System.Boolean)` | Determines whether the given exception should trigger a retry attempt according to minimal YDB retry strategy rules. The logic closely follows the official YDB SDK: it considers transient statuses and certain service-level timeouts. |  |

| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.Types.TypeWrapper.CompiledWrappers` | Provides access to delegates, created from expressions, defined in wrapper class using following property and type mappings, configured for : private static IEumerable<T> Wrappers { get; } where T could be or Tuple<LambdaExpression, bool>. Boolean flag means that mapping expression compilation allowed to fail if it is set to . This could be used to map optional API, that present only in specific versions of provider. If wrapper doesn't need any wrapper delegates, this property could be ommited. |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.Builder.CteAnnotationsContainer

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: CteAnnotationsContainer, Cte, Annotations, Container.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.Builder.CteAnnotationsContainer` | Immutable carrier for the state produced by the callback. Placed into the query expression tree as a single constant so linq2db's query-cache equality comparer can discriminate distinct AsCte(b => ...) configurations: reference-equal delegate values would otherwise collapse to the same cache entry. The constructor snapshots the caller's annotation bag into an internally-owned store, so post-call mutations of the source cannot affect translation or cache keying. |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.ColumnReaderAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ColumnReaderAttribute, Column, Reader, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.ColumnReaderAttribute` | Attribute specify parameter index of reader column ordinal for custom reader method that accepts reader and column ordinal parameters. This information is used by mapper builder for queries with behavior enabled. Alternative approach (when possible) is to move reading of raw value from reader to external expression and passing it to custom reader. // such helper requires attribute to work in SequentialAccess mode [ColumnReader(1)] static int GetCustomInt(DbDataReader rd, int ordinal) => int.Parse(rd.GetString(ordinal)); ReaderExpressions[...] = (rd, i) => GetCustomInt(rd, i); // such helper will work without attribute static int GetCustomInt(string value) => int.Parse(value); ReaderExpressions[...] = (rd, i) => GetCustomInt(rd.GetString(i)); |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.EagerEvaluationAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: EagerEvaluationAttribute, Eager, Evaluation, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Internal.Linq.EagerEvaluationAttribute` | Marks an -returning extension method whose call should be evaluated on the client during expression tree exposure, regardless of the shape of its source argument. The returned queryable's expression replaces the original method call before query translation. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.BuildCteHeaderHint(LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.CteClause)` | Emitted between the CTE name / column list and the opening parenthesis of the CTE body. Default implementation emits AS MATERIALIZED / AS NOT MATERIALIZED when the corresponding support flag is and a annotation is present on the CTE. Providers with non-ANSI hint syntax (e.g. Oracle /*+ MATERIALIZE */) override this method fully. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlBuilder.FakeTable` | If set to false and provider doesn't support SELECTs without FROM clause, this property should contain name of table (or equivalent SQL) with single record. IMPORTANT: as this property could return SQL, we don't escape it, so it should contain only valid SQL/identifiers. |  |

|---|---|---|

### LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlOptimizer.SeparateDistinctFromPagination

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: SeparateDistinctFromPagination, Separate, Distinct, From, Pagination, DISTINCT, ORDER, SELECT, TAKE, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.BasicSqlOptimizer.SeparateDistinctFromPagination(LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SqlStatement,System.Func{LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery,System.Boolean})` | Moves Distinct query into another subquery. Useful when preserving ordering is required, because some providers do not support DISTINCT ORDER BY. -- before SELECT DISTINCT TAKE 10 c1, c2 FROM A ORDER BY c1 -- after SELECT TAKE 10 B.c1, B.c2 FROM ( SELECT DISTINCT c1, c2 FROM A ) B ORDER BY B.c1 |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsOuterExpressionInAggregate

Kind: Property.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.AcceptsOuterExpressionInAggregate` | Provider supports aggregated expression with Outer reference SELECT ( SELECT SUM(inner.FieldX + outer.FieldOuter) FROM table2 inner ) AS Sum_Column FROM table1 outer Otherwise aggeragated expression will be wrapped in subquery and aggregate function will be applied to subquery column. SELECT ( SELECT SUM(sub.Column) FROM ( SELECT inner.FieldX + outer.FieldOuter AS Column FROM table2 inner ) sub ) AS Sum_Column FROM table1 outer Default (set by ): . |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAccessBuggyLeftJoinConstantNullability

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsAccessBuggyLeftJoinConstantNullability` | Indicates that provider has bug in LEFT join translator. In the following example can_be_null is always not null, which is wrong. SELECT can_be_null FROM Some LEFT JOIN 1 as can_be_null, * FROM Other ON ... As workaround translator is trying to check for nullability all projected fields. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsComplexJoinConditionSupported

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsComplexJoinConditionSupported` | When disabled, all conditions from INNER JOIN ON moved to WHERE except conjunction of equality predicates. FROM T1 INNER JOIN T2 ON t1.field1 == t2.field1 AND t1.field2 == t2.field2 AND t1.field3 > 10 -- with flag: FROM T1 INNER JOIN T2 ON t1.field1 == t2.field1 AND t1.field2 == t2.field2 WHERE t1.field3 > 10 Default: . |  |

|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlProvider.SqlProviderFlags.IsMultiTablesSupportsJoins` | Indicates that provider supports JOINS in FROM clause which have several tables SELECT ... FROM table1 INNER JOIN query ON ... , table2 Otherwise the following query will be generated: SELECT ... FROM ( SELECT ... FROM table1, table2 ) S INNER JOIN query ON ... , FROM table2 Default: . Currently not supported only by Access. |  |

After modification statement is equivalent symantically. --before SELECT c1, c2 FROM A -- after SELECT B.c1, B.c2 FROM ( SELECT c1, c2 FROM A ) B |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.QueryHelper.WrapQuery``2(``1,``0,System.Func{``1,LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery,LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.IQueryElement,System.Boolean},System.Action{``1,LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery,LinqToDB.Internal.SqlQuery.SelectQuery},System.Boolean,System.Boolean)` |  |  |

Keeps columns count the same. After modification statement is equivalent semantically. --before SELECT c1, c2 -- QA FROM A -- after (with 2 subqueries) SELECT C.c1, C.c2 -- QC FROM ( SELECT B.c1, B.c2 -- QB FROM ( SELECT c1, c2 -- QA FROM A ) B FROM ) C |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: NoLinqCache, Linq, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` | Provides a scope, in which LINQ queries will not be added to a LINQ query cache. This could be used to tell linq2db to not cache queries that operate with big parametes. More details could be found here. Take into account that this class only disables adding of new query, created in its scope, to a cache. If query already present in cache - linq2db will use cached query. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``1}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. | Group=NavigationLoading; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=JoinGraph; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``1}},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{System.Linq.IQueryable{``1},System.Linq.IQueryable{``1}}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. parameter could be used to define additional association loading logic like filters or loading of more associations. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``1}}},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{System.Linq.IQueryable{``1},System.Linq.IQueryable{``1}}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. parameter could be used to define additional association loading logic like filters or loading of more associations. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWithAsTable

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: LoadWithAsTable, Load, With, Table, Group=NavigationLoading, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=JoinGraph, Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText, Provider=ProviderDefined.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWithAsTable``1(LinqToDB.ITable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Object}})` | Specifies associations, that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. Some usage examples: // loads records from Table1 with Reference association loaded for each Table1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.Reference); // loads records from Table1 with Reference1 association loaded for each Table1 record // loads records from Reference2 association for each loaded Reference1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.Reference1.Reference2); // loads records from Table1 with References collection association loaded for each Table1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.References); // loads records from Table1 with Reference1 collection association loaded for each Table1 record // loads records from Reference2 collection association for each loaded Reference1 record // loads records from Reference3 association for each loaded Reference2 record // note that a way you access collection association record (by index, using First() method) doesn't affect // query results and always select all records db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.References1[0].References2.First().Reference3); | Group=NavigationLoading; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=JoinGraph; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CompareNulls, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL, SELECT, FROM, WHERE, INNER, JOIN, AND, NOT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls` | If set to nullable fields would be checked for IS NULL in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. is a backward compatible option that works mostly as , but sniffs parameters value and changes = into IS NULL when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into IS NULL. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: . public class MyEntity { public int? Value; } db.MyEntity.Where(e => e.Value != 10) from e1 in db.MyEntity join e2 in db.MyEntity on e1.Value equals e2.Value select e1 var filter = new [] {1, 2, 3}; db.MyEntity.Where(e => ! filter.Contains(e.Value)) Would be converted to next queries under : SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR Value != 10 SELECT e1.Value FROM MyEntity e1 INNER JOIN MyEntity e2 ON e1.Value = e2.Value OR (e1.Value IS NULL AND e2.Value IS NULL) SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR NOT Value IN (1, 2, 3) |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ConcatenateOrderBy, Concatenate, Order, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple or calls: - if - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Default.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default` | Gets default instance. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DisableQueryCache, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: . It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: EnableContextSchemaEdit, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit` | If , user could add new mappings to context mapping schems (). Otherwise, will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single instance once, configure mappings for it and use this instance for all context instances. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateExpressionTest, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to linq2db subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GuardGrouping, Guard, Grouping, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if - will be thrown for such queries; - if - eager loading used. Default value: . More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IgnoreEmptyUpdate, Ignore, Empty, Update.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate` | Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if - will be thrown. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: KeepDistinctOrdered, Keep, Distinct, Ordered, SQL, SELECT, DISTINCT, FROM, ORDER, GROUP.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered` | Allows SQL generation to automatically transform SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: OptimizeJoins, Optimize, Joins, SQL, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins` | If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: . |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ParameterizeTakeSkip` | Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: . |  |

Search anchors: PreferApply, Prefer, Apply, CROSS, APPLY, OUTER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferApply` | Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreferExistsForScalar, Prefer, Exists, For, Scalar, EXISTS, SELECT, FROM, WHERE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar` | If , EXISTS operator will be generated instead of IN operator for scalar values. SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE EXISTS(SELECT * FROM MyEntity2 e2 WHERE e2.Value = e.Value) vs SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE Value IN (SELECT Value FROM MyEntity2 e2) Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreloadGroups, Preload, Groups, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group . Default value: . |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.TraceMapperExpression` | Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See for more details. Default value: . |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: AssociationAttribute, Association, Attribute, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute` | Defines relation between tables or views. Could be applied to: - instance properties and fields; - instance and static methods. For associations, defined using static methods, mapping side defined by type of first parameter. Also, optionally, you can pass data context object as extra method parameter. Based on association type - to one or to multiple records - result type should be target record's mapping type or collection. By default associations are used only for joins generation in LINQ queries and will have value for loaded records. To load data into association, you should explicitly specify it in your query using method. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AliasName, Alias, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName` |  |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationSetterExpression, Association, Setter, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression` | Specifies a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Action takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> setContainerValue; setContainerValue = (container, value) => container.Value = value; |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationSetterExpressionMethod, Association, Setter, Expression, Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod` | Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Result of method should be Action which takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. public class SomeEntity { [Association(SetExpressionMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> OtherImpl() { return (container, value) => container.Value = value; } } |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CanBeNull, Can, Null.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull` | Defines type of join: - inner join for CanBeNull = false; - outer join for CanBeNull = true. When using Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata, the default value for associations (cardinality 1) is derived from nullability. Otherwise the default value is (for collections and when option is disabled). |  |

This predicate will be used together with / join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result. |  |

Search anchors: OtherKey, Other, Key.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` | Gets or sets comma-separated list of association key members on another side of association. Those keys will be used for join predicate generation and must be compatible with keys. You must specify keys it you do not use custom predicate (see ). |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Predicate.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate` | Specifies predicate expression. This predicate will be used together with / join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: QueryExpression, Query, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression` | Specifies query expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Lambda function takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> associationQuery; associationQuery = (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id); |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: QueryExpressionMethod, Query, Expression, Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod` | Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns IQueryable expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Result of query method should be lambda which takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. public class SomeEntity { [Association(ExpressionQueryMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> OtherImpl() { return (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id); } } |  |

Currently this attribute has several issues: - you can apply it to class or interface - such attribute will be ignored by linq2db; - it is possible to define attribute without setting value; - you can define alias to another alias property or field and potentially create loop. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName` | Gets or sets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column mapped to a property or field of composite object, should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } [Column("city", "Residence.City")] [Column("user_name", "Name")] public class User { public string Name; [Column("street", ".Street")] [Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")] public Address Residence { get; set; } } |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MemberName, Member, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName` | Gets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column is mapped to a property or field of composite object, should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } [Column("city", "Residence.Street")] [Column("user_name", "Name")] public class User { public string Name; [Column("street", ".Street")] [Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")] public Address Residence { get; set; } } |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.IGenericInfoProvider` | Generic conversions provider. Implementation class must be generic, as type parameters will be used for conversion initialization in method. // this conversion provider adds conversion from IEnumerable<T> to ImmutableList<T> for specific T type parameter class EnumerableToImmutableListConvertProvider<T> : IGenericInfoProvider { public void SetInfo(MappingSchema mappingSchema) { mappingSchema.SetConvertExpression<IEnumerable<T>,ImmutableList<T>>( t => ImmutableList.Create(t.ToArray())); } } for more details. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: MapValueAttribute, Map, Value, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute` | Defines bidirectional mapping between enum field value, used on client and database value, stored in database and used in queries. Enumeration field could have multiple attributes. Mapping from database value to enumeration performed when you load data from database. Linq2db will search for enumeration field with with required value. If attribute with such value is not found, you will receive error. If you cannot specify all possible values using , you can specify custom mapping using methods like . Mapping from enumeration value performed when you save it to database or use in query. If your enum field has multiple attributes, you should mark one of them as default using property. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute.Constructor

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Constructor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.ScalarTypeAttribute` | Overrides default scalar detection for target class or structure. By default linq2db treats primitives and structs as scalar types. This attribute allows you to mark class or struct as scalar type or mark struct as non-scalar type. Also see . Note that if you marks some type as scalar, you will need to define custom mapping logic between object of that type and data parameter using methods. |  |

| `F:LinqToDB.Sql.DateParts.Week` | This date part behavior depends on used database and also depends on where if calculated - in C# code or in database. Eeach database could have own week numbering logic, see notes below. Current implementation uses following schemas per-provider: C# evaluation: CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.Calendar.GetWeekOfYear(date.Value, CalendarWeekRule.FirstDay, DayOfWeek.Sunday) Databases: US numbering schema used by: MS AccessSQL CESQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASEInformixYdbUS 0-based numbering schema used by MySQL databaseISO numbering schema with incorrect numbering of first week used by SAP HANA databaseISO numbering schema with proper numbering of first week used by: FirebirdPostgreSQLClickHousePrimitive (each 7 days counted as week) numbering schema: DB2OracleSQLite numbering logic cannot be classified by human being |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsPure, Pure, SQL, ORDER, NOT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure` | If , it notifies SQL Optimizer that expression returns same result if the same values/parameters are used. It gives optimizer additional information how to simplify query. For example ORDER BY PureFunction("Str") can be removed because PureFunction function uses constant value. For example Random function is NOT Pure function because it returns different result all time. But expression is Pure in case of executed query. is also Pure function because it returns the same result with the same parameters. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.Ordinal

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Ordinal, LINQ, ORDER, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.Ordinal``1(``0)` | Forces LINQ translator to generate column ordinal for column (1-base column index in select statement). Currently it is supported only for ORDER BY clause. Can be used in LINQ query as top level order expression. For example the following two similar queries query = query .OrderBy(x => Sql.Ordinal(x.Field2)) .ThenBy(x => x.Field2); query = from q in query orderby Sql.Ordinal(q.Field2), q.Field1 select q; Should generate thw following SQL: SELECT t.Field1, t.Field2 FROM SomeTable t ORDER BY 2, t.Field1 |  |

| `P:LinqToDB.SqlOptions.GenerateFinalAliases` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: . For the query var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; When property is SELECT [child].[ChildID] as [TrackId] FROM [Child] [child] Otherwise alias will be removed SELECT [child].[ChildID] FROM [Child] [child] |  |
